classification.loc_class: QC - Physics: plasma and electromagnetic fields

theory.canonical_statement: ID3827 records Mu Haar Hmagnetic Relation as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `D:\00_Engine\CSV\refactored_derivations.tex` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: \[Mu]Haar]; Hmagnetic= DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[n], {n, 0, Infinity}]]; Hphoton = DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[{k, \[Sigma]}], {k, MomentumSpace}, {\[Sigma], {L, R}}]]; Htotal = TensorProduct[Hgyro, Hmagnetic, Hphoton]; \end{implementation} \subsection{Tunneling Hamiltonian and Instanton Action} \begin{definition}[Tunneling Hamiltonian] Define the tunneling Hamiltonian acting on the flux degree of freedom by \[ H_{\text{tunnel}} = \hbar\omega_0\,a^\dagger a + \frac{\hbar^2}{2m_{\text{eff}}}\,\partial_d^2 + V(d) + g_{\text{tunnel}}\,(a^\dagger + a)(\partial_d + \partial_d^\dagger), \]
canonical_equation.source_section_latex: \[Mu]Haar]; Hmagnetic= DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[n], {n, 0, Infinity}]]; Hphoton = DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[{k, \[Sigma]}], {k, MomentumSpace}, {\[Sigma], {L, R}}]]; Htotal = TensorProduct[Hgyro, Hmagnetic, Hphoton]; \end{implementation} \subsection{Tunneling Hamiltonian and Instanton Action} \begin{definition}[Tunneling Hamiltonian] Define the tunneling Hamiltonian acting on the flux degree of freedom by \[ H_{\text{tunnel}} = \hbar\omega_0\,a^\dagger a + \frac{\hbar^2}{2m_{\text{eff}}}\,\partial_d^2 + V(d) + g_{\text{tunnel}}\,(a^\dagger + a)(\partial_d + \partial_d^\dagger), \]

id_system.loc_class: QC - Physics: plasma and electromagnetic fields

wolfram_form.existing_from_tex: \[Mu]Haar]; Hmagnetic= DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[n], {n, 0, Infinity}]]; Hphoton = DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[{k, \[Sigma]}], {k, MomentumSpace}, {\[Sigma], {L, R}}]]; Htotal = TensorProduct[Hgyro, Hmagnetic, Hphoton]; \end{implementation} \subsection{Tunneling Hamiltonian and Instanton Action} \begin{definition}[Tunneling Hamiltonian] Define the tunneling Hamiltonian acting on the flux degree of freedom by \[ H_{\text{tunnel}} = \hbar\omega_0\,a^\dagger a + \frac{\hbar^2}{2m_{\text{eff}}}\,\partial_d^2 + V(d) + g_{\text{tunnel}}\,(a^\dagger + a)(\partial_d + \partial_d^\dagger), \]

blocks.wolfram_input: HoldForm[TZPIDCanonicalLatex["ID3827", 1, "\\[Mu]Haar]; Hmagnetic= DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[n], {n, 0, Infinity}]]; Hphoton = DirectSum[Table[BasisVector[{k, \\[Sigma]}], {k, MomentumSpace}, {\\[Sigma], {L, R}}]]; Htotal = TensorProduct[Hgyro, Hmagnetic, Hphoton]; \\end{implementation} \\subsection{Tunneling Hamiltonian and Instanton Action} \\begin{definition}[Tunneling Hamiltonian] Define the tunneling Hamiltonian acting on the flux degree of freedom by \\[ H_{\\text{tunnel}} = \\hbar\\omega_0\\,a^\\dagger a + \\frac{\\hbar^2}{2m_{\\text{eff}}}\\,\\partial_d^2 + V(d) + g_{\\text{tunnel}}\\,(a^\\dagger + a)(\\partial_d + \\partial_d^\\dagger), \\]"]]
